---
title: "Posit Publisher — Community Issue Tracker"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import json
import os
import requests
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from IPython.display import Markdown, HTML
import humanize

NOW = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
REPO = "posit-dev/publisher"
API_BASE = "https://api.github.com"

GITHUB_TOKEN = os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN", "").strip()
HEADERS = {"Accept": "application/vnd.github+json"}
if GITHUB_TOKEN:
    HEADERS["Authorization"] = f"token {GITHUB_TOKEN}"


def gh_paginate(url, params=None):
    """Fetch all pages from a GitHub API endpoint."""
    results = []
    params = params or {}
    params.setdefault("per_page", 100)
    while url:
        resp = requests.get(url, headers=HEADERS, params=params)
        if not resp.ok:
            detail = resp.json() if resp.headers.get("content-type", "").startswith("application/json") else resp.text
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"GitHub API {resp.status_code} for {url}\n"
                f"Token present: {bool(GITHUB_TOKEN)} (length {len(GITHUB_TOKEN)})\n"
                f"Response: {detail}"
            )
        results.extend(resp.json())
        url = resp.links.get("next", {}).get("url")
        params = {}  # params are baked into the 'next' URL
    return results


def fetch_issues():
    """Fetch all community-labeled issues with their comments."""
    issues = gh_paginate(
        f"{API_BASE}/repos/{REPO}/issues",
        params={"labels": "community", "state": "all", "per_page": 100},
    )
    # GitHub's issues endpoint also returns PRs — filter them out
    issues = [i for i in issues if "pull_request" not in i]

    # Fetch comments for each issue
    for issue in issues:
        if issue.get("comments", 0) > 0:
            comments = gh_paginate(issue["comments_url"])
            issue["_comments"] = comments
        else:
            issue["_comments"] = []
    return issues


def fetch_timeline(issue_number):
    """Fetch timeline events for an issue to detect cross-referenced PRs."""
    try:
        events = gh_paginate(
            f"{API_BASE}/repos/{REPO}/issues/{issue_number}/timeline",
        )
        return [
            e for e in events
            if e.get("event") in ("cross-referenced", "referenced")
        ]
    except Exception:
        return []


raw = fetch_issues()

# Build a flat dataframe
rows = []
for issue in raw:
    author = issue["user"]["login"]
    comments = issue.get("_comments", [])
    created = pd.Timestamp(issue["created_at"])

    # Determine author association for the issue itself
    issue_author_assoc = issue.get("author_association", "")

    # Find first team response (MEMBER, COLLABORATOR, or OWNER)
    first_team_response = None
    for c in comments:
        assoc = c.get("author_association", "")
        if assoc in ("MEMBER", "COLLABORATOR", "OWNER"):
            first_team_response = pd.Timestamp(c["created_at"])
            break

    label_names = [l["name"] for l in issue.get("labels", [])]
    assignees = [a["login"] for a in issue.get("assignees", [])]

    rows.append({
        "number": issue["number"],
        "title": issue["title"],
        "state": issue["state"].upper(),
        "author": author,
        "created": created,
        "closed": pd.Timestamp(issue["closed_at"]) if issue["closed_at"] else pd.NaT,
        "first_response": first_team_response,
        "response_time": (first_team_response - created) if first_team_response else pd.NaT,
        "comment_count": len(comments),
        "last_comment_at": pd.Timestamp(comments[-1]["created_at"]) if comments else pd.NaT,
        "labels": label_names,
        "assignees": assignees,
    })

df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
df = df.sort_values("created", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: summary-banner
open_count = (df["state"] == "OPEN").sum()
closed_count = (df["state"] == "CLOSED").sum()
total = len(df)

awaiting = df[(df["state"] == "OPEN") & df["first_response"].isna()]
awaiting_count = len(awaiting)

one_week_ago = NOW - timedelta(days=7)
new_this_week = df[df["created"] >= one_week_ago]
new_count = len(new_this_week)

responded = df[df["first_response"].notna()]
if len(responded) > 0:
    median_response = responded["response_time"].median()
    median_str = humanize.naturaldelta(median_response)
else:
    median_str = "N/A"

HTML(f"""
<div style="display:flex; gap:1rem; flex-wrap:wrap; margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <div style="flex:1; min-width:150px; background:#f0f7ff; border-left:4px solid #2563eb; padding:1rem; border-radius:6px;">
    <div style="font-size:2rem; font-weight:700; color:#2563eb;">{new_count}</div>
    <div style="color:#555;">New this week</div>
  </div>
  <div style="flex:1; min-width:150px; background:#fef3f2; border-left:4px solid #dc2626; padding:1rem; border-radius:6px;">
    <div style="font-size:2rem; font-weight:700; color:#dc2626;">{open_count}</div>
    <div style="color:#555;">Open</div>
  </div>
  <div style="flex:1; min-width:150px; background:#f0fdf4; border-left:4px solid #16a34a; padding:1rem; border-radius:6px;">
    <div style="font-size:2rem; font-weight:700; color:#16a34a;">{closed_count}</div>
    <div style="color:#555;">Closed</div>
  </div>
  <div style="flex:1; min-width:150px; background:#fefce8; border-left:4px solid #ca8a04; padding:1rem; border-radius:6px;">
    <div style="font-size:2rem; font-weight:700; color:#ca8a04;">{awaiting_count}</div>
    <div style="color:#555;">Awaiting response</div>
  </div>
  <div style="flex:1; min-width:150px; background:#faf5ff; border-left:4px solid #9333ea; padding:1rem; border-radius:6px;">
    <div style="font-size:2rem; font-weight:700; color:#9333ea;">{median_str}</div>
    <div style="color:#555;">Median response time</div>
  </div>
</div>
""")
```

## New Issues This Week

Issues with the **community** label created in the last 7 days.

```{python}
#| label: new-issues
if len(new_this_week) == 0:
    display(Markdown("_No new community issues this week._"))
else:
    new_display = new_this_week[["number", "title", "author", "created", "first_response"]].copy()
    new_display["created"] = new_display["created"].dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    new_display["first_response"] = new_display["first_response"].apply(
        lambda x: x.strftime("%Y-%m-%d") if pd.notna(x) else "—"
    )
    new_display["number"] = new_display["number"].apply(
        lambda n: f'<a href="https://github.com/{REPO}/issues/{n}" target="_blank">#{n}</a>'
    )
    new_display.columns = ["Issue", "Title", "Author", "Created", "First Response"]

    display(HTML(new_display.to_html(index=False, escape=False, classes="table table-striped table-sm")))
```

## Engagement: Are We Responding Promptly?

Tracking how quickly community issues receive a first team response helps us ensure reporters feel heard.

```{python}
#| label: response-times
import plotly.express as px

responded_df = df[df["first_response"].notna()].copy()
responded_df["response_hours"] = responded_df["response_time"].dt.total_seconds() / 3600

fig = px.bar(
    responded_df.sort_values("created"),
    x=responded_df.sort_values("created")["number"].apply(lambda n: f"#{n}"),
    y="response_hours",
    color="response_hours",
    color_continuous_scale=["#16a34a", "#ca8a04", "#dc2626"],
    labels={"x": "Issue", "response_hours": "Hours to First Response"},
    title="Time to First Team Response (hours)",
)
fig.add_hline(y=24, line_dash="dash", line_color="red", annotation_text="24h target")
fig.update_layout(
    showlegend=False,
    xaxis_title="Issue",
    yaxis_title="Hours",
    height=400,
    coloraxis_showscale=False,
)
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: awaiting-response
if len(awaiting) > 0:
    display(Markdown("### Issues Still Awaiting a First Response"))
    display(Markdown("These community issues have **no team reply yet** — they may need attention."))

    awaiting_display = awaiting[["number", "title", "author", "created"]].copy()
    awaiting_display["age"] = awaiting_display["created"].apply(
        lambda x: humanize.naturaldelta(NOW - x)
    )
    awaiting_display["created"] = awaiting_display["created"].dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    awaiting_display["number"] = awaiting_display["number"].apply(
        lambda n: f'<a href="https://github.com/{REPO}/issues/{n}" target="_blank">#{n}</a>'
    )
    awaiting_display.columns = ["Issue", "Title", "Author", "Created", "Age"]

    display(HTML(awaiting_display.to_html(index=False, escape=False, classes="table table-striped table-sm")))
else:
    display(Markdown("_All community issues have received at least one team response._"))
```

## Closing the Loop: Are Resolved Issues Being Communicated?

When a fix is merged, we should follow up on the original issue — either closing it or commenting to let the reporter know the fix is available. This section identifies open issues that may have linked PRs already merged.

```{python}
#| label: close-the-loop
open_issues = df[df["state"] == "OPEN"].copy()

loop_rows = []
for _, row in open_issues.iterrows():
    events = fetch_timeline(row["number"])
    has_pr_ref = len(events) > 0

    if pd.notna(row["last_comment_at"]):
        days_since_activity = (NOW - row["last_comment_at"]).days
    else:
        days_since_activity = (NOW - row["created"]).days

    loop_rows.append({
        "number": row["number"],
        "title": row["title"],
        "created": row["created"],
        "has_pr_reference": has_pr_ref,
        "days_since_activity": days_since_activity,
        "assignees": ", ".join(row["assignees"]) if row["assignees"] else "—",
    })

loop_df = pd.DataFrame(loop_rows)

if len(loop_df) > 0:
    stale_threshold = 14
    stale = loop_df[loop_df["days_since_activity"] >= stale_threshold]
    pr_linked = loop_df[loop_df["has_pr_reference"]]

    if len(pr_linked) > 0:
        display(Markdown("### Issues With Linked PR References"))
        display(Markdown("These open issues have cross-referenced PRs — check if the fix has been merged and the issue can be closed."))

        pr_display = pr_linked[["number", "title", "days_since_activity", "assignees"]].copy()
        pr_display["number"] = pr_display["number"].apply(
            lambda n: f'<a href="https://github.com/{REPO}/issues/{n}" target="_blank">#{n}</a>'
        )
        pr_display.columns = ["Issue", "Title", "Days Since Activity", "Assignee"]
        display(HTML(pr_display.to_html(index=False, escape=False, classes="table table-striped table-sm")))

    if len(stale) > 0:
        display(Markdown(f"### Stale Issues (No Activity in {stale_threshold}+ Days)"))
        display(Markdown("These open community issues haven't seen any activity recently and may need follow-up."))

        stale_display = stale[["number", "title", "days_since_activity", "assignees"]].copy()
        stale_display["number"] = stale_display["number"].apply(
            lambda n: f'<a href="https://github.com/{REPO}/issues/{n}" target="_blank">#{n}</a>'
        )
        stale_display.columns = ["Issue", "Title", "Days Since Activity", "Assignee"]
        display(HTML(stale_display.to_html(index=False, escape=False, classes="table table-striped table-sm")))

    if len(pr_linked) == 0 and len(stale) == 0:
        display(Markdown("_All open issues look healthy — no stale issues or unresolved PR links._"))
```

## Full Issue List

```{python}
#| label: full-list
full = df[["number", "title", "state", "author", "created", "first_response", "comment_count", "assignees"]].copy()
full["created"] = full["created"].dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
full["first_response"] = full["first_response"].apply(
    lambda x: x.strftime("%Y-%m-%d") if pd.notna(x) else "—"
)
full["assignees"] = full["assignees"].apply(lambda a: ", ".join(a) if a else "—")
full["number"] = full["number"].apply(
    lambda n: f'<a href="https://github.com/{REPO}/issues/{n}" target="_blank">#{n}</a>'
)
full.columns = ["Issue", "Title", "State", "Author", "Created", "First Response", "Comments", "Assignees"]

display(HTML(full.to_html(index=False, escape=False, classes="table table-striped table-sm")))
```

---

<small>Report generated `{python} NOW.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")` from [posit-dev/publisher](https://github.com/posit-dev/publisher) issues labeled **community**.</small>
